prompt:
PR 1 of the Umbrella App rewrite (docs/UMBRELLA-PLAN.md). This is SCAFFOLDING ONLY â€” do not port any existing feature logic. That is PRs 2-4.

## What to build

Replace the current vanilla-JS PWA (app.js, timer.js, style.css, index.html at repo root) with a Vite + Preact + TypeScript + Tailwind scaffold that boots three empty tabs plus a Settings tab, wraps in Capacitor for Android, and ships an LLM provider stub.

### Web app skeleton

- Stack: Vite + Preact + TypeScript + Tailwind. No router, no state library, no UI component library. See UMBRELLA-PLAN.md "Stack" section for the guardrails â€” follow them strictly. `preact` not `react`. Use `@preact/preset-vite`.
- Source layout:
  - `src/main.tsx` â€” Preact entry, reads `window.__entry` (set by Capacitor, default 'flux' on web) to pick initial tab.
  - `src/App.tsx` â€” shell with bottom tab bar: Workouts, Meditate, Check-in, Settings. Plain `useState` for active tab + conditional render.
  - `src/tabs/Workouts.tsx`, `src/tabs/Meditate.tsx`, `src/tabs/Checkin.tsx`, `src/tabs/Settings.tsx` â€” each a placeholder component that just shows the tab name. Settings should have a placeholder section for 'LLM provider' (just a heading + 'not configured' for now).
  - `src/llm/index.ts` â€” exports `generate({prompt, schema?, stream?, capabilities?})` and `useLLM()` hook returning `{ generate, available, configure }`. Default provider returns `available: false` and `generate()` throws 'LLM not configured'. Real providers come later.
  - `src/db/` â€” empty placeholder dir with a README noting per-tab IndexedDB stores (`vibe-db`, `flux-db`, `balance-db`) will be added in feature PRs.
- Tailwind: standard `tailwind.config.js` + `postcss.config.js` + `@tailwind base/components/utilities` in `src/index.css`. Dark mode class strategy. No design system yet â€” utility classes inline are fine.
- TypeScript: `tsconfig.json` set up for Preact JSX (`jsxImportSource: 'preact'`). Strict mode on.
- HTML: replace root `index.html` with Vite's standard `<div id="app">` + module script entry.

### Capacitor Android shell

- Install `@capacitor/core`, `@capacitor/cli`, `@capacitor/android`. Add `@capacitor/haptics` and `@capacitor/app` as initial plugins (will be used in later PRs but install now so the shell is complete).
- `capacitor.config.ts`:
  - `appId: 'dev.gunk.flux'`
  - `appName: 'Flux'`
  - `webDir: 'dist'`
- Run `npx cap add android` to scaffold the `android/` directory. Commit the generated files.
- Modify `android/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml` to add THREE `<activity-alias>` entries pointing at `MainActivity`, each with its own `android:icon`, `android:label`, and `MAIN`/`LAUNCHER` intent-filter. Use placeholder icons (just reuse the default mipmap launcher icon for all three for now â€” distinct icons come in later PRs). Aliases: `FluxLauncher` (label 'Flux'), `VibeLauncher` (label 'Vibe'), `BalanceLauncher` (label 'Balance'). Each alias has a `<meta-data android:name="entry" android:value="flux|vibe|balance"/>`.
- Set `MainActivity` to `launchMode="singleTask"`.
- `MainActivity.java` (or .kt): extends `BridgeActivity`. Override `onCreate` and `onNewIntent` to read the activity-alias `entry` meta-data, then inject `window.__entry = '<value>'` into the WebView before/at load. Standard Capacitor approach is to set it via `bridge.getWebView().evaluateJavascript()` on page-finished, OR inject a script tag into the bundle before load. Pick the cleaner one and document the choice in a code comment.

### Build + dev

- `npm run dev` â€” Vite dev server.
- `npm run build` â€” outputs `dist/`.
- `npx cap sync android` then `npx cap run android` â€” builds and launches APK (you won't be able to actually run an emulator in the agent env, but the gradle build must succeed: `cd android && ./gradlew assembleDebug` should produce an APK).
- Verify the gradle build succeeds before opening the PR.

### Deploy preservation

The CURRENT deploy is **GitHub Pages**, not fly.io/Caddy (the umbrella plan is wrong on this detail). The repo has `.github/workflows/pages.yml` and `preview.yml` that upload the repo root as the Pages artifact. Update both workflows so they:
1. Run `npm ci && npm run build` (inside the nix dev shell â€” see CLAUDE.md, nix is mandatory)
2. Upload `dist/` as the Pages artifact instead of the repo root.

Keep test.yml working â€” it currently runs Playwright. The existing Playwright tests in `tests/` reference the old app and WILL break with the rewrite. Delete the old tests and add ONE new Playwright smoke test: `tests/smoke.spec.ts` that loads the dev/preview server, asserts all four tab buttons render, clicks each, and verifies the tab content updates. Use `@playwright/test`.

### Nix flake

Update `flake.nix`:
- `devShells.default` already has `nodejs_20` â€” keep it. Add nothing else for now (Capacitor's Android build needs JDK + Android SDK, but the agent won't be able to install Android SDK in a CI/nix-friendly way easily; do the gradle build manually via the local Android SDK if available, otherwise document that `./gradlew` requires a host-installed Android SDK and that the gradle build is verified locally not in CI for this PR).
- Update `packages.default` derivation: replace the `cp index.html style.css app.js` install phase with `npm ci && npm run build && cp -r dist/* /`. Use `buildNpmPackage` if it makes the derivation simpler.
- Keep `devShells.test` (Playwright) intact, but update the `npm install` line to work with the new `package.json`.

### Files to delete (wholesale replacement per the plan)

- `app.js`, `timer.js`, `style.css` at repo root
- The old `index.html` (replaced by Vite's)
- `data/` â€” old static seed data, no longer used. Move anything load-bearing into `src/tabs/Workouts.tsx` as a TODO comment; the actual port happens in PR 2.
- Old `tests/` â€” replaced by single smoke test
- Old root `manifest.json` â€” Vite's PWA setup will handle this later; for now, leave a minimal one that Vite serves.

### What MUST work before opening the PR

1. `nix develop --command npm run dev` boots Vite on localhost, all four tabs render and switch.
2. `nix develop --command npm run build` produces a `dist/` dir.
3. `nix develop .#test --command npx playwright test` â€” smoke test passes against the built output.
4. `cd android && ./gradlew assembleDebug` succeeds (if Android SDK is available in the agent env; if not, explicitly note it in the PR description and call this out as 'verified locally only').
5. The GH Pages workflow YAML is updated and lints (`actionlint` if available, otherwise just YAML-valid).

### Constraints from CLAUDE.md (read it before starting)

- Never reference Claude/AI in commit messages or PR titles/descriptions.
- All work in a feature branch + PR. Never push to main, never merge.
- Always use nix (`nix develop --command ...`) â€” not raw `npm install`.

### PR description

Reference the umbrella plan and call out:
- This is PR 1 of 5.
- What is INTENTIONALLY NOT done (no feature logic, no distinct launcher icons yet, no real LLM providers, no IndexedDB stores).
- The Pages workflow change (so reviewer knows the deploy artifact path changed).
- Whether the Android gradle build was verified in this environment or only documented.
